steps.description: Rust integration test for Lock flow
steps.command: cd src/api/api && SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo test --test sequence_e2e_test seq1_lock -- --nocapture
steps.expected: Test exits with code 0; stdout confirms: lock record created in DB, SR_0 computed via SHA3-256("QS_LOCK_V1" || chain_id || asset || amount || dest_addr || expiry || nonce || pk_dilithium), and lockWithSR0 transaction submitted to L1 Vault at 0x6F889C00a5e674ab0b9403AfBa0fBEbe30511c67 on Sepolia; no panic or FAILED lines present

steps.description: Playwright integration spec for Consumer Lock
steps.command: cd src/frontend/web && npx playwright test e2e/integration/lock.integration.spec.ts --reporter=json
steps.expected: All Playwright test cases pass (status: passed) with zero failures; JSON reporter output confirms UI lock creation transitions to a success state and a corresponding DB row is observable; no test is skipped or timed out

steps.description: Verify lock row was created and pk_dilithium populated
steps.command: psql "$DATABASE_URL" -t -A -c "SELECT lock_id, status, length(pk_dilithium) FROM locks ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;"
steps.expected: Exactly one row is returned; status column value is either 'pending' or 'locked'; length(pk_dilithium) equals exactly 1952 bytes

steps.description: Verify totalLocked increased on Sepolia Vault
steps.expected: Returned uint256 value is strictly greater than 0 wei; the call must succeed without revert; the value must reflect the locked amount from the most recent Lock sequence execution

acceptance_criteria: Backend integration test exits with code 0 and stdout confirms SR_0 computation and L1 submission with no FAILED or panic output
Playwright lock.integration.spec.ts reports all tests passed with zero failures and zero skips
A row exists in the locks table with status equal to 'locked' and pk_dilithium byte length equal to exactly 1952
Sepolia Vault totalLocked() returns a value strictly greater than 0 wei
No silent fallback or hardcoded address appears in the execution trace
